metadata.description: Admin password for VM serial console access (always set alongside SSH key)
SSH public key for VM authentication (preferred over password)
VPN shared key for all S2S connections
Deploy the SRE Agent for automated incident detection and troubleshooting
SRE Agent access level: High (Contributor) or Low (Reader)
SRE Agent mode: Review (propose+approve), Autonomous, or ReadOnly
Microsoft Entra group ID whose members can manage the SRE Agent (required when deploySreAgent=true)
Routes for spoke prefixes to program on GatewaySubnet RT
Static private IP for the NVA LB frontend (must be in NVA subnet)
Routes for the NVA subnet (cross-hub spoke prefixes via remote NVA LB)
Private Endpoint subnet prefix (/24), empty to skip PE subnet

variables.$fxv#0:
#cloud-config
package_update: true
packages:
  - iptables
  - iptables-persistent
  - dnsmasq
write_files:
  - path: /etc/sysctl.d/99-ip-forward.conf
    content: |
      net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
    owner: root:root
    permissions: '0644'
  - path: /etc/dnsmasq.d/azure-dns.conf
    content: |
      # Forward all DNS queries to Azure DNS
      server=168.63.129.16
      # Listen on all interfaces (spokes and on-prem use NVA as DNS)
      listen-address=0.0.0.0
      bind-interfaces
      # Don't read /etc/resolv.conf (avoid loops)
      no-resolv
      # Cache DNS responses
      cache-size=1000
    owner: root:root
    permissions: '0644'
runcmd:
  - sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-ip-forward.conf
  # Stateful firewall: use connection tracking so asymmetric routing breaks traffic
  - iptables -P FORWARD DROP
  - iptables -A FORWARD -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
  - iptables -A FORWARD -m conntrack --ctstate NEW -j ACCEPT
  - iptables -A FORWARD -m conntrack --ctstate INVALID -j DROP
  # SNAT for outbound Internet traffic (non-RFC1918 destinations)
  - iptables -t nat -N INTERNET_SNAT || true
  - iptables -t nat -A INTERNET_SNAT -d 10.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN
  - iptables -t nat -A INTERNET_SNAT -d 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN
  - iptables -t nat -A INTERNET_SNAT -d 192.168.0.0/16 -j RETURN
  - iptables -t nat -A INTERNET_SNAT -j MASQUERADE
  - iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -j INTERNET_SNAT
  - mkdir -p /etc/iptables
  - iptables-save > /etc/iptables/rules.v4
  # DNS proxy: disable systemd-resolved to free port 53, then start dnsmasq
  - systemctl disable --now systemd-resolved || true
  - rm -f /etc/resolv.conf
  - echo "nameserver 168.63.129.16" > /etc/resolv.conf
  - systemctl enable --now dnsmasq

osProfile.linuxConfiguration: [if(variables('useSSHKey'), createObject('disablePasswordAuthentication', empty(parameters('adminPassword')), 'ssh', createObject('publicKeys', createArray(createObject('path', format('/home/{0}/.ssh/authorized_keys', parameters('adminUsername')), 'keyData', parameters('adminPublicKey'))))), createObject('disablePasswordAuthentication', false()))]

metadata.description: Hub VNet resource ID for peering
Hub NVA LB frontend IP for UDR next hop
Additional routes beyond default route (other spokes, on-prem, etc.)
Custom DNS server IPs for the spoke VNet (empty = Azure default)

variables.$fxv#0:
#cloud-config package_update: true packages: - curl - traceroute - net-tools - inetutils-ping - dnsutils - nmap - tcpdump - iperf3 - apache2 write_files: - path: /var/www/html/index.html permissions: '0644' content: | Azure SRE Agent - Test App

Hello from Azure SRE Agent Test Environment

Hostname: HOSTNAME_PLACEHOLDER

Purpose: Networking SRE Agent fault injection testing

runcmd: - hostnamectl set-hostname $(hostname) || true - sed -i "s/HOSTNAME_PLACEHOLDER/$(hostname)/" /var/www/html/index.html - systemctl enable apache2 - systemctl start apache2

resources.name: [format('{0}/{1}', format('{0}-{1}-vnet', parameters('prefix'), parameters('spokeName')), format('{0}-to-{1}', parameters('spokeName'), parameters('hubVnetName')))]

metadata.description: Private Endpoint subnet ID (must have privateEndpointNetworkPolicies enabled)
VNet IDs to link to the Private DNS Zone (hub VNets only)

properties.scriptContent:
echo 'SRE Health Probe

OK

Private Endpoint connectivity verified.

' > index.html az storage blob service-properties update --account-name $1 --static-website --index-document index.html --404-document index.html --auth-mode login az storage blob upload --account-name $1 --container-name '$web' --name index.html --file index.html --overwrite --auth-mode login

metadata.description: Traffic Manager FQDN for HTTP reachability test
Storage Account static website FQDN for Private Endpoint HTTP probe

metadata.description: Connection Monitor resource ID to monitor

properties.description: Connection Monitor: more than 20% of probes are failing (sustained over 5 min)
Connection Monitor: one or more tests report unreachable destination sustained over 5 min (TestResult=Fail)
Connection Monitor: average round-trip time exceeds 1000ms (informational — VPN paths have higher latency)

metadata.description: Azure region — must be eastus2, swedencentral, or australiaeast
Access level for the agent: High (Reader+Contributor) or Low (Reader only)
Agent mode: Review (propose+approve), Autonomous, or ReadOnly
Microsoft Entra group ID for initial sponsor group (required for agent identity). Members of this group can manage the agent.
Resource group IDs the agent should monitor (full resource IDs)
Log Analytics workspace ID for the agent App Insights
Principal ID of the SRE Agent managed identity
